channel.title: Korea Startup Thesis
channel.description: Weekly deep dives into Korean startups for global VC and angel investors — data-driven analysis, founder stories, and the signals you won't find anywhere else.

item.title: Rebellions: The Ex-SpaceX Engineer Who Came Back to Korea to Challenge Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Korea Startup Thesis
item.description: Rebellions is a Korean fabless AI semiconductor startup that has built an inference-only ASIC delivering 20.7% better power efficiency than Nvidia's H200. Five years after founding: $850M raised, $2.34B valuation, ARM's first startup investment in APAC. This issue covers the full story — founder background, technology deep dive, competitive moat analysis, risks, and what to watch.
item.content:encoded:
Issue #001 — Company Deep Dive

Nvidia's GPUs have a critical weakness: energy inefficiency at the inference stage. Rebellions is a fabless semiconductor startup that has relentlessly targeted that single problem.

  • Total raised: $850M
  • Pre-IPO valuation: $2.34B
  • Power efficiency vs. H200: +20.7%
  • Inference market 2030E: $475B

Founder Park Sung-hyun: KAIST → MIT PhD → Intel Labs → Samsung → SpaceX → Morgan Stanley VP → Rebellions (2020)

Read the full deep dive →
